title: SANS and magnetometry study of the magnetic phase diagram of the B20 helimagnet FeRhSi
authors: E. V. Altynbaev, A. V. Guseva, D. O. Skanchenko, V. N. Krasnorussky, A. V. Bokov, D. A. Salamatin, V. A. Sidorov, A. V. Tsvyashchenko
abstract: This manuscript reports the first direct neutron-scattering evidence for long-period helimagnetism in the newly identified 4d-substituted B20 compound Fe0.5Rh0.5Si. By combining SANS with low-field magnetometry, we establish the magnetic modulation, construct a field-temperature phase diagram, and identify a candidate A-phase region supported by an independent structural signature. The work is important beyond this single compound because it expands the family of chiral B20 helimagnets into Rh-substituted materials, where spin-orbit coupling, disorder, and Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interactions can be tuned. It will interest researchers in chiral magnetism, topological spin textures, magnetic neutron scattering, and quantum materials, and provides a foundation for future studies of emergent magnetic phases and topology-driven phenomena in chemically tuned chiral magnets.
summary: This paper reports the first direct neutron scattering evidence of long-period helimagnetism in Fe0.5Rh0.5Si, a B20 compound. By combining SANS measurements with low-field magnetometry, the authors construct a detailed field-temperature phase diagram and identify a candidate A-phase region. The work advances the understanding of tunable chiral magnets by linking magnetic anomalies to structural signatures.
main_problem: The study aims to map out the magnetic phase diagram of the B20 helimagnet Fe0.5Rh0.5Si and characterize a candidate A-phase region within it.
main_result: The research successfully combined SANS measurements confirming long-period helical modulation (lambda_h ~ 79 nm) with magnetometry to define a field-temperature phase diagram, supporting the existence of a candidate A-phase region.
method: The study employed a combination of low-field magnetometry (measuring M(H), dM/dH, and d^2M/dH^2) and Small-Angle Neutron Scattering (SANS) on polycrystalline samples.
model_or_system: The physical system is the 4d-substituted B20 helimagnet Fe0.5Rh0.5Si, which exhibits magnetic ordering driven by crystallographic chirality and Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interactions.
key_observables: Magnetic modulation wave vector (k_s), helical period (lambda_h), differential susceptibility (dM/dH), second derivative of magnetization (d^2M/dH^2), and field-dependent SANS intensity maxima.
important_parameters: Characteristic fields ($H_{c1}, H_{c1m}, H_{c2}$); ordering temperatures derived from magnetometry ($\sim 71 	ext{ K}$) and SANS ($\sim 70 	ext{ K}$).
main_assumptions: The candidate A-phase region is not treated as an unambiguous thermodynamic phase boundary, and the interpretation relies on combining data from polycrystalline samples and transverse SANS geometry.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate M(H) isotherms showing magnetic anomalies; Figure 2 details diagnostic plots of susceptibility and curvature for the candidate A-phase; Figure 3 presents the combined H-T phase diagram incorporating field boundaries and the candidate region.
paper_structure: The paper systematically uses magnetometry to define low-field phase transitions ($H_{c1}, H_{c1m}, H_{c2}$), then utilizes SANS to provide direct, complementary evidence of the underlying long-period helical magnetic structure, culminating in a combined phase diagram.
why_interesting: The work provides empirical evidence on tuning chiral magnetism using 4d substitutions (Rh) and directly probes emergent topological spin textures via neutron scattering techniques. This is highly relevant for understanding complex magnetic ground states beyond simple mean-field models.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
